title: Timely Harvesting
summary: Harvest immediately at physiological maturity to prevent field mold.
steps: Harvest when dry during sunny hours.
Avoid dropping cobs on wet soil.
safety_notes: Do not harvest during heavy rain.
scaling_tip: Organize community labor groups for rapid harvesting.
evidence_note: Extended field drying increases insect and Aspergillus exposure.

title: Tarp/Canvas Drying
summary: Never dry grain directly on bare soil.
steps: Lay down a clean, waterproof tarpaulin.
scaling_tip: Cooperatives can bulk-buy tarps to reduce costs.
evidence_note: Soil is the primary reservoir for A. flavus spores.

title: Safe Sorting (Culling)
summary: Remove broken, discolored, or insect-damaged grains.
safety_notes: Do NOT feed moldy culls to chickens or cows. Remove from food/feed chain.
scaling_tip: Use mechanized sorters at aggregation level.
evidence_note: Broken grains have compromised pericarps, making them highly susceptible to Aspergillus.

title: Pallet Usage (Dunnage)
summary: Keep stored bags off the floor to prevent moisture wicking.
steps: Place wooden pallets on storage floor.
safety_notes: Ensure pallets are dry and termite-free.
scaling_tip: Local carpenters can make low-cost dunnage from offcuts.
evidence_note: Ground moisture transfer is a leading cause of localized storage mold.

title: Moisture Testing & Safe Threshold
summary: Ensure grain is adequately dried to stop fungal synthesis.
steps: Perform a manual salt-bottle test or use a moisture meter.
Ensure grain moisture drops below the safe threshold.
safety_notes: Use only clean, dry testing containers.
scaling_tip: Train lead farmers in moisture validation methods.
evidence_note: If grain cannot be dried below the safe moisture threshold of 13.5% within 48 hours of harvest, fungal synthesis accelerates.

title: Ventilation Check
steps: Ensure eaves are open but screened.
Check for 'sweating' on tin roofs in mornings.
scaling_tip: Retrofit existing stores with screened vents.
evidence_note: Condensation under tin roofs causes re-wetting of upper grain bags.

title: Hermetic Storage Bags
summary: Use airtight bags to kill pests and stop mold growth.
steps: Fill inner liner, squeeze out air, tie tightly.
safety_notes: Grain MUST be completely dry before sealing to prevent rotting.
scaling_tip: Subsidize hermetic bags through cooperative loans.
evidence_note: Hermetic bags halt insect metabolism, reducing heat and moisture generation.

title: Safe Feed Handling
summary: Protect dairy cows from contaminated feed.
steps: Store dairy meal in dry, raised areas.
Never use rejected human food as dairy feed.
safety_notes: Aflatoxin in feed metabolizes to AFM1 in milk within 12 hours.
scaling_tip: Integrate feed safety into dairy coop extension.
evidence_note: Feeding moldy grain to animals directly introduces AFM1 into milk, contributing to childhood stunting.

title: Transport Hygiene
summary: Ensure trucks and carts don't contaminate clean grain.
steps: Check for holes in tarps used for covering.
Avoid transporting during heavy rain without covers.
safety_notes: Do not transport grain with toxic chemicals.
scaling_tip: Enforce transport hygiene at aggregation gates.
evidence_note: Transport delays in open trucks during rain frequently lead to acute mold outbreaks.

title: Insect Pest Management
summary: Reduce insect damage to prevent fungal entry points.
steps: Scout for pests like Fall Armyworm.
safety_notes: Follow safe handling for any treatments.
evidence_note: Pests physically damage the protective pericarp of the grain, creating direct entry wounds for Aspergillus spores.

title: Market Segregation
summary: Protect dry grains from wet batches at the market.
safety_notes: Mixing wet and dry grain ruins the entire batch.
scaling_tip: Market committees should enforce segregation protocols.
evidence_note: One damp bag can raise the relative humidity of an entire storage stack.

title: Mechanical Shelling Safety
steps: Calibrate shellers to avoid cracking kernels.
Ensure grain is dry enough to shell easily.
safety_notes: Keep hands clear of moving parts.
scaling_tip: Train youth operators on machine calibration.
evidence_note: Cracked grains are highly susceptible to fungal infection.

title: Aggregation First-In-First-Out
summary: Manage stock rotation to prevent old stock deterioration.
scaling_tip: Digital inventory tracking for large aggregators.
evidence_note: Prolonged storage increases the probability of moisture absorption and fungal bloom.

title: Milk Hygiene Check
summary: Basic hygiene prevents bacterial pathogens (does not remove toxins).
steps: Use aluminum or food-grade plastic cans, not jerricans.
safety_notes: Boiling milk does NOT destroy aflatoxin M1.
scaling_tip: Subsidize aluminum cans via dairy coops.
evidence_note: High AFM1 prevalence in Kenyan milk is directly tied to poor on-farm feed handling.

title: Safe Disposal Protocols
summary: What to do with heavily moldy grain.
steps: Do NOT feed to livestock or poultry.
Do NOT burn (inhaling smoke can be toxic).
Remove from food/feed chain. Follow county agriculture/public health guidance for disposal.
safety_notes: Wear a mask when handling heavily molded grain.
scaling_tip: Counties need centralized safe disposal coordination.
evidence_note: Burning toxic grain is hazardous. Remove bad grain safely from the food chain.

title: Mechanical Drying Access
summary: Use forced-air drying when weather prevents sun drying.
steps: Ensure grain reaches safe storage moisture.
safety_notes: Verify facility is operational before transport.
scaling_tip: Group transport reduces per-kg drying costs.
evidence_note: NCPB charges Ksh 18.90 per 1% moisture drop; highly cost-effective against total crop loss.
